what_it_does: Stores a neutral, file-local interpretive summary of the claude-ds analysis of pitch-deck.md, cataloguing its extracted design DNA, token recalibration recommendations, guardrails, and open questions in a fixed ledger schema.
observations: It is a JSON object with a fixed schema: project, path, model, what_it_does, observations, standouts, conventions, concerns, notes, questions, purpose_vs_actual, apparent_themes, intention_signals, novelty, contribution, summary_for_embedding, and intention_for_embedding.

It documents a three-pass material analysis of a 17-page pitch deck, extracting frame/grid ratios, a near-white zoning ladder, a gold-to-bronze-to-tan ramp, a type system, an atom catalogue, 13 layout archetypes, a soft z-stack, narrative architecture, and rules between elements.
It treats the deck as a baseline vocabulary against which other folders are tested, and replaces property nouns with generic roles to stay content-agnostic.
It includes sampled hex values derived via hue clustering and recommends recalibrating live tokens such as --accent-gold and --zone-* toward the sampled reality.
It captures an explicit ABSENT-list of guardrails/anti-patterns: no pure black, no hard shadows, no gradients outside photos, no left-accent-border cards, no emoji.
It records inferred intention signals, novelty, and contribution, plus two embedding-ready summaries.
It flags its own limitations: sampled hexes are approximations, token edits should await cross-folder confirmation, and the archetype catalogue may over-fit one deck.
It notes that each analysis pass routes findings into SYSTEM-GAPS.md.
standouts: Baseline self-declaration: it sets the vocabulary all other folders are tested against and remains content-agnostic.
Zoning is built from undertone, texture, and emboss rather than hue; the warm/neutral pairing is the semantic signal and is 'felt not seen'.
Colour-role logic maps ink to content, gold to active/decision, and bronze to structural/quiet connective tissue; contrast itself is used as a hierarchy tool.
Static slides encode latent motion: arrows cross split dividers, off-frame bleed implies continuation, and implied entrance order equals reading order.
An explicit ABSENT-list is captured as guardrails rather than as a simple absence of features.
Pixel-consistent margins across 17 slides imply a single master template, and embedded screenshots carry an app component vocabulary such as status pills and nav.
conventions: Fixed ledger schema with interpretive fields.
Ratios-not-pixels: positions and sizes recorded as percentages of frame.
Content-agnostic extraction with property nouns replaced by generic roles.
Evidence-grounded claims backed by hue clustering and measured positions.
Separates constant DNA from variable expression as axis=f(content,position).
Token-recalibration discipline: recommends editing token values in place.
Fail-loud-on-drift: routes gaps to SYSTEM-GAPS.md rather than silently absorbing them.
Neutral, file-local tone with no cross-file ranking.
concerns: Sampled hex values are approximations read from rendered images via hue clustering, not authoritative source-token definitions.
It recommends recalibrating live system tokens based on a single baseline folder, though it defers final promotion to multi-folder confirmation.
The 13 layout archetypes and atom catalogue may over-fit one 17-page deck before cross-folder testing.
Intention signals, themes, and novelty are inferred rather than explicitly stated by the source material.
Embedding summaries compress nuanced caveats about approximation and confirmation.
notes: A neutral, file-local interpretive ledger entry that reverse-engineers a pitch deck's generative design DNA into ratios, sampled values, and guardrails while flagging where cross-folder confirmation is still needed.
questions: How accurate are the hue-clustered sampled hexes versus the original source files' true colour values?
Are the recommended token recalibrations meant to be applied from this baseline alone or only after cross-folder confirmation?
Is the green/amber/red status set a defined semantic set in the source, or inferred from a few embedded UI screenshots?
What is the exact visual form of the 'frame signature' and 'invisible 4-corner frame' in the source?
How are the 13 layout archetypes named or instantiated in code or components?
What is the precise relationship between this file and SYSTEM-GAPS.md / PROGRESS.md?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — it states it is the baseline folder that sets the vocabulary all others are tested against, and it delivers an exhaustive three-pass extraction including recalibration recommendations and guardrails.
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to reverse-engineer the single generative system behind a polished deck into recomputable, content-agnostic rules so that confirmed universals can later be promoted into the design system's tokens, primitives, and components rather than copied as a visual style.
novelty: It samples real pixel values to correct existing tokens, names zoning as a sub-perceptual undertone signal, maps ramp position to perceived loudness, reads static slides as encoding their own motion, and codifies deliberate absence as an explicit guardrail set.
contribution: It contributes a structured baseline vocabulary a fused system would keep: frame ratios and frame signature, a near-white zoning ladder with sampled values, a gold-to-bronze-to-tan ramp, ink/gold/bronze colour-role logic, an atom catalogue, 13 layout archetypes, a modular type scale, a layered soft z-stack, a latent-motion cue set, and an ABSENT-list of guardrails, together with explicit concerns about approximation and confirmation.
summary_for_embedding: Neutral interpretive ledger entry for a three-pass baseline analysis of a 17-page pitch deck, capturing frame ratios, undertone zoning, a gold-bronze-tan ramp, type system, atom catalogue, layout archetypes, z-stack, colour-role logic, latent-motion cues, and ABSENT-list guardrails as recomputable design DNA.
intention_for_embedding: To record a file-local, evidence-grounded reverse-engineering of the deck's generative design system so confirmed universals can later be promoted into tokens, primitives, and components without premature ranking or cross-folder claims.
